user_outcome: A Planner can run the dedicated ambiguity check over typed, workspace-confined references, while every generic quality check emits the canonical check identity and the retired generic maintainability path cannot dispatch.
rationale: This slice owns C-02. S02 updates only canned generic-check responses so they satisfy the exact v0.15 schema; this slice still owns production requested/emitted check matching, mismatch rejection, ambiguity separation, and retired-maintainability dispatch suppression. The ambiguity model may see only the reviewed public spec and explicitly referenced public technical artefacts; resolution is local, one-level, deterministic, path-confined, and completed before dispatch. It must never read credentials, follow a symlink outside the workspace, fetch the network, or expose file bytes in errors. Output is stable JSON or plain text with no interactive UI, and resolution/sorting is O(reference count plus referenced bytes).
in_scope: Resolve only spec.references entries in the three C-02 forms, first duplicate-safe parse and validate the reviewed spec, then obtain its physical workspace root, require the reviewed spec at the exact canonical docs/release/<release>/<slice>/spec.json path beneath that root, and validate spec.release plus spec.slice_id before resolving any artefact.

Apply planning/normative-clarifications.md section 3 exactly: unsafe reviewed-spec, canonical-source, lexical-path, and physical-confinement failures stop before dispatch; safe content-resolution failures render the exact UNRESOLVED reason for the ambiguity model in deterministic order.
Render each unique resolved artefact once in bytewise repo-relative path order with its verbatim UTF-8 bytes and no recursive reference discovery or network access.
Parse spec-ambiguity output only as spec-ambiguity-report-v1, reject duplicate raw JSON keys or a fingerprint present in both finding maps, and derive FAIL exactly from non-empty blocking_findings.

Require each of the other five llm-check-report-v1 outputs to carry the requested canonical check identity, reject mismatch before reporting PASS, and make generic maintainability-review return non-zero guidance to sworn maintainability review with zero dispatch or mutation.

Keep the CLI and MCP adapters thin over one resolver/check authority and preserve verdict plus exit-code equivalence for PASS, blocking FAIL, malformed output, configuration error, and reference error.
out_of_scope: Maintaining report lifecycle, semantic scope, commits, or pushes belongs to S07 through S13; this slice performs a stateless Planner ambiguity check only.
The dedicated sworn maintainability review and adjudicate commands are implemented by S13-maintainability-engine-cutover.
Automatic migration or rewriting of a spec with ambiguity findings belongs to the human Planner and S17's ratified-delta application, not the model check.
References found only in rationale, scope, acceptance text, touchpoints, test_refs, or a referenced artefact are deliberately ignored by C-02.

effort_complexity.rationale: The slice coordinates strict filesystem confinement, duplicate-safe JSON, two distinct report schemas, model-dispatch suppression, CLI exit behavior, and MCP parity across thirteen files.

risks.description: A lexical path that looks confined resolves through a symlink outside the repository and leaks data to the model.
risks.mitigation: Resolve the physical workspace root and every physical target, require a strict beneath-root relation after symlink evaluation, and prove an external canary is never read or dispatched.

risks.description: The ambiguity response is parsed through the generic schema and loses its fingerprint-keyed disposition semantics.
risks.mitigation: Use a distinct parser and exact embedded schema selected only for CheckSpecAmbiguity; cross-schema fixtures and duplicate-key mutations must fail closed.

risks.description: A check-type mismatch or retired maintainability spelling dispatches the wrong prompt under a trusted label.
risks.mitigation: Bind requested check identity before dispatch and emitted check identity after parse; hard-reject maintainability on CLI, gate, and MCP paths with a counting fake proving zero calls.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the built sworn llm-check command runs spec-ambiguity for a canonical slice containing file, sibling-slice, and contract references THE SYSTEM SHALL resolve exactly C-02, supply each unique confined artefact once, validate spec-ambiguity-report-v1, and pair the derived verdict with exit 0 only for PASS (N-02, N-10, N-11).

IF duplicate-safe reviewed-spec/schema validation, physical workspace-root discovery, canonical source-path agreement, lexical path validity, or physical confinement fails in that order THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return respectively the first applicable C-02 class reviewed-spec-schema-invalid, workspace-root-unavailable, reviewed-spec-source-path-mismatch, reference-path-invalid, or reference-path-escape, exit non-zero, and make zero model calls; IF a safely confined reference is missing, non-regular, unreadable, invalid UTF-8/JSON/schema, release-mismatched, contract-count-invalid, or slice-ID-mismatched THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL render the exact ordered UNRESOLVED reason to the ambiguity model, whose blocking finding keeps the final exit non-zero.

IF a spec-ambiguity response has duplicate raw keys, appears to satisfy llm-check-report-v1 instead, repeats a fingerprint across blocking and advisory maps, or states a verdict inconsistent with blocking_findings THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL fail closed as a contract error.

WHEN any non-ambiguity generic check completes THE SYSTEM SHALL require its emitted check identity to equal the requested check, and a missing, unknown, or mismatched identity SHALL produce non-zero regardless of the model's stated PASS.

WHEN CLI, gate, or MCP receives the retired generic maintainability-review check THE SYSTEM SHALL exit or return non-success with guidance to sworn maintainability review, perform zero dispatches, and write no record.
